behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "before any downstream processing" and "listing all missing items." 
Slot B: Validator-to-orchestrator gate (module handoff) is the failure boundary. 
Slot C: A Unit test of only field-check logic misses accidental downstream invocation. 
Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; (b) no real AI output dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no model decision governing handoff. Rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is local gating + error aggregation only. Label: Integration per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Wire validator + intake orchestrator with spies/fakes for insurance/triage/scheduling; submit payload missing multiple required fields; assert returned error enumerates all missing fields and assert zero downstream calls occurred.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "rejects future dates... more than 150 years ago... non-date strings." 
Slot B: Pure date-parsing/range logic in one validator boundary. 
Slot C: Higher-level wiring tests are unnecessary to expose these parsing/range failures. 
Rejected Integration+ because behavior text contains no module handoff, infra, or model dependency. Label: Unit per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Test DOB validator function directly with table-driven invalid inputs (future, >150 years old, malformed string) and valid boundary dates; assert exact rejection outcomes.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "calls an external payer API" and must send exact fields + interpret statuses. 
Slot B: Real HTTP contract boundary to external API is the failure boundary. 
Slot C: Mocked client can hide real serialization/header/path mismatches and status/body handling differences. 
Rejected Agentic/Workflow because payer API is deterministic non-LLM integration per behavior text. Label: System per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real HTTP test server (or payer sandbox) using the production HTTP stack; verify outbound request contains exact patient ID/plan code and that real HTTP responses for eligible/not eligible/pending map to expected internal states.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "payer API is unreachable" plus continue intake. 
Slot B: Network-failure behavior at real HTTP boundary drives the failure mode. 
Slot C: Simple mocks often raise generic errors and miss true timeout/connection-refused handling paths. 
Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI decision quality is involved. Label: System per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real connection failure/timeout against the insurance endpoint using production HTTP client settings; assert eligibility becomes pending and orchestration continues to subsequent steps.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "invocation includes ... symptoms and ... emergency rubric in the model input payload." 
Slot B: Handoff from intake data/config module into triage request builder is the boundary. 
Slot C: Unit on prompt-builder alone can miss wiring bugs where orchestrator omits rubric/symptoms. 
Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network required if payload capture stub used; (b) behavior tests request construction, not model judgment; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no model decision needed for assertion. Rejected Agentic by text focusing on input payload content only. Label: Integration per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage service with deterministic model stub that captures inbound payload; assert reported symptoms and configured rubric are both present and correctly mapped.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency" for emergency combinations. 
Slot B: Correctness depends on real model clinical-priority judgment. 
Slot C: Stubbed/model-mocked responses always pass and cannot reveal misclassification. 
Rejected Workflow because this is one model decision, not multiple agentic stages. Label: Agentic per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Send real triage requests with emergency symptom sets to the live model repeatedly; assert urgency is high with acceptance thresholds and track variance/failures.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field." 
Slot B: Parser-to-persistence handoff is the key boundary. 
Slot C: Unit parser tests can miss schema/repository mapping drops where explanation is not persisted. 
Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no real AI-quality dependence if response fixture used; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no model decision governs handoff correctness here. Rejected Agentic because behavior checks storage presence, not explanation quality. Label: Integration per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response fixture containing score + explanation through parser and repository layer; assert persisted record includes non-empty clinician-readable explanation field.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "live AI ... explanation cites case-specific symptoms ... not generic boilerplate." 
Slot B: Explanation quality/relevance depends on real model generation behavior. 
Slot C: Deterministic stubs cannot validate whether generated rationale is case-specific. 
Rejected Workflow because this is a single generation-quality check, not multi-agentic sequencing. Label: Agentic per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live triage calls on diverse symptom inputs; evaluate returned explanations for explicit mention of input symptoms and concrete urgency rationale, rejecting generic templated text.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "first available slot" with urgency-to-time-window policy. 
Slot B: Scheduler logic + calendar repository interaction is the boundary. 
Slot C: Unit-only rule tests can miss query/order bugs in repo handoff. 
Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no real AI output required if urgency is test input; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no model decision governs handoff in this behavior. Rejected System because local DB/module wiring is sufficient per behavior text. Label: Integration per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with representative availability; run scheduler for high/medium urgency and assert selected slot is first valid slot and satisfies same-day/48h constraints.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "does not double-book ... when two patients are processed concurrently." 
Slot B: Concurrency control across scheduler + shared calendar DB transaction boundary. 
Slot C: Single-thread Unit tests miss race/locking conflicts that appear only with concurrent module interaction. 
Integration brake: (a) no mandatory external OS/network/subprocess beyond local DB concurrency; (b) no AI-output dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no model decision handoff. Rejected System because failure can be reproduced with in-process concurrent workers + local DB. Label: Integration per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same candidate slot against local calendar DB with real transaction semantics; assert exactly one booking succeeds and no duplicate slot rows exist.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "confirmation message includes ... fields ... instructions appropriate to visit type." 
Slot B: Template/rendering logic boundary in message composer. 
Slot C: Higher-level tests are not required to reveal omitted fields or wrong template branch. 
Rejected Integration+ because behavior text is content composition logic without required infra/model dependencies. Label: Unit per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation formatter with fixture inputs for multiple visit types; assert rendered message includes required fields and correct preparation instruction variant.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "pipeline orchestration ... completes correctly" with "deterministic stubbed triage output." 
Slot B: Cross-module state handoff through all local stages is the boundary. 
Slot C: Unit tests per stage miss orchestration/order/state propagation failures. 
Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required if dependencies are stubbed/local; (b) behavior explicitly uses deterministic stubbed triage, disqualifying real model-quality need; (c) no multiple real agentic steps; (d) LLM-involved stage is stubbed, so no model decision governs correctness here. Rejected Agentic/Workflow due explicit "deterministic stubbed triage output" text. Label: Integration per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process pipeline with stubbed insurance and deterministic triage outputs plus local scheduler/confirmation modules; assert stage order, state transitions, and final confirmation payload are correct.

behaviors.description: The full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "full live intake pipeline" includes live AI triage and external insurance call. 
Slot B: A real model call is in the execution path; completion depends on actual model output being usable. 
Slot C: Stubbed triage can hide failures where live model output format/content breaks downstream automation. 
Rejected Workflow because behavior specifies one agentic step (triage) inside broader pipeline, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence. Label: Agentic per Slot B and Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a real submission through production-like pipeline using live payer API path and live triage model; assert unattended completion through scheduling and confirmation with no manual correction.
